best_practices: AskUserQuestion conditional template lines must be enclosed in braces and end with the (if <condition>) suffix INSIDE the braces, e.g. {- do X (if use_merge_queue == true)}. A bare conditional line without the (if ...) suffix is rendered UNCONDITIONALLY by the template engine (the condition is silently ignored). Mirror the existing pattern when authoring branch-conditional prompt/gate templates.
insights: phase-3-outline assessment_coverage Q-Gate: a downstream-propagation deliverable that lists external consumer-repo paths (e.g. /other-repo/.plan/marshal.json) as affected files cannot carry CERTAIN_INCLUDE assessments — the assessment flow is architecture-bound to the plan-marshall worktree and only assesses in-worktree files. Those external paths are legitimate propagation targets per .plan/downstream_propagation.md, so the resulting assessment_coverage gap is a known false positive to resolve as taken_into_account, not a missing assessment requiring re-outline.

Self-review (q-gate) findings are a standing consideration project-wide: across the archived-plan corpus they are consistently folded into the work (taken_into_account) rather than suppressed — expect q-gate findings to be resolved in-flight, not deferred or dismissed.

User gate-feedback is consistently actioned in this project: across the corpus user-review findings are folded into the work (taken_into_account) rather than suppressed — user feedback at gates is a standing consideration that gets resolved, not deferred.

A recurring subset of q-gate findings are accepted as deliberate, tolerated tradeoffs (expected false-positives) rather than fixed — consistent with the documented expected-FP q-gate lessons; when a q-gate finding is a known structural false positive, accepting it is an established disposition.

phase-1-init sibling-collision detection is a point-in-time snapshot. It cannot see a sibling PR that MERGES during this plan's execution and resolves the same target as one of this plan's deliverables. The compensating catch point is the phase-6 finalize-step-sync-baseline semantic re-verify gate, where the now-redundant deliverable surfaces as a rebase content-conflict and is dropped. Expect the mid-execution-sibling-overtook-a-deliverable pattern to be caught at sync-baseline rather than at init. Treat a sync-baseline rebase conflict on a deliverable's exact target as the signal to re-verify whether that deliverable is still needed.

The Claude Code harness externally kills run_in_background jobs with zero output and no ledger row at ANY tier, including orchestrator-tier detached finalize builds. First observed live on an orchestrator-tier verify module-tests job mid-finalize, plan background-build-kill. The shipped recovery contract works: classify the killed zero-byte outcome via manage-change-ledger classify-outcome scoped by worktree-sha, then recover via Monitor or foreground re-run. Never blind re-background a killed zero-byte job.

The project treats automated review-bot meta comments as non-actionable: review-summary rollups that only re-list their own inline findings, and 'review finished' command acknowledgements, are dispositioned without a fix task. Triage effort belongs on the inline findings such comments aggregate, not on the aggregate itself.
key_dependencies_reasoning: pyproject.toml dependency set: pyprojectx orchestrates pytest+mypy+ruff for the Python test/lint pipeline

marketplace.targets.description: Multi-target adapter system that exports bundles to alternative AI-assistant formats (currently Claude Code native + OpenCode). Implements TargetBase plus per-target frontmatter mapping and body transforms.

test.description: Top-level Python test tree mirroring marketplace bundle layout (one subdir per bundle). Holds pytest modules that execute marketplace scripts via subprocess + PYTHONPATH and parse their TOON outputs.

purpose_reasoning: Top-level pyproject with Python 3.12 requirement; hosts test entry points executed via pyprojectx (./pw module-tests / verify)
responsibility: Root project module housing the pyprojectx-based test harness for the entire marketplace. Contains the unified pytest suite that exercises every bundle's scripts (plan-marshall, pm-dev-*, pm-documents, pm-plugin-development, pm-requirements) plus the multi-target adapter generator.
responsibility_reasoning: source: pyproject.toml + test/ directory layout (per-bundle test folders + sync-plugin-cache)

skills_by_profile_reasoning: general-dev: cross-cutting baseline; default module covers infra-level tests + adapter without language-specific bundle binding; Batch: marshall-plugin build_system detected; all marketplace bundles host skills/scripts authored under plugin-architecture standards
tips: Whole-tree coverage is an orchestrator-tier build (~37min instrumented) that the harness kills, and the build-pyproject per-module coverage wrapper timeout (~123s) is too short for instrumented whole-tree runs. Defer whole-tree coverage to CI verify rather than running it locally. Scope local coverage to a single module when a local run is needed.

Color-suppression env pins are mirrored at two sites: the executor template env-prep block (tools-script-executor/templates/execute-script.py.template) and plugin-doctor's _color_suppressed_env helper (pm-plugin-development .../plugin-doctor/scripts/_analyze_manage_invocation.py). Keep the full variable set synchronized across both, since Python 3.14 argparse colorization poisons downstream text parsers: PYTHON_COLORS=0, NO_COLOR=1, pop FORCE_COLOR, CLICOLOR=0, CLICOLOR_FORCE=0, PY_COLORS=0.

The default pseudo-module owns marketplace/targets and test/marketplace/targets but has NO bundle directory, so per-module verify scoping cannot target it. verify default fails deterministically with Error Bundle not found marketplace/bundles/default. A plan touching marketplace/targets code cannot locally scope-verify that module. Its tests run only via a whole-tree verify pass or in CI. Do not hand back pending_verify_modules listing default expecting a scoped verify to succeed.

Ruff isort in this repo sets combine-as-imports = false, so an aliased member import (from X import Y as Z) must live in its own from-statement and cannot be combined with unaliased members of the same module. Combining them fails the quality-gate isort check.
